review_title: ASP.NET is the best technology for building web applications
description: My first professional web application I built, I used asp.net framework to develop it. My experience was somehow out of this world. It was my first time building a web application using ASP.NET, I had to learn from the beginning how the framework works, and once I figured out how it works, I fell in love with it; 2 applications after the first one was developed using it.
author: Marenyane M.
pros: I like the fact that it is free I like the fact that it very secure You can build web applications with ease For Microsoft developers, it makes it easy to develop web applications running on Microsoft platform
cons: I don't like the fact that it is not a very easy technology to use It is not easy to learn like PHP

review_title: Zero to MVP fast-track
description: I've used it for years and actively work two saas platforms based on it.
author: Chris N.
pros: This is a great framework to go from zero to MVP very quickly. Constantly progressing and updating. Can be of great value to get a new solution to market as quickly as possible.
cons: The worst think about ANZ spawns from one of its strengths. The constant progress of the framework means major shifts, especially on the UI side, can be daunting.

review_title: Fraudulent
description: Very bad do not complete with your license, because you do not have the right to update your project to the last version you were entitled to.
author: Fred P.
pros: The best thing really is the back-end and the ability to self-generate code.
cons: that you are entitled for a year to the updated versions, but the year is over and you can no longer download your latest version to which you were entitled so they block you and in the end it is a theft.

review_title: Rapid development
description: We chose this software because it has accelerated the development of our crm
author: Gianluca C.
pros: We like Asp.net Power Tools. We use this tool to create our own tables
cons: There are no things that have disappointed us in a particular way

review_title: ASP.NET for Desktop browser insurance app
description: The system allows sellers of building insurance to instantly calculate the annual premium based on value, age, etc. of the building.The ready-to-use client => controller => service => DB flow with all the surrounding authentication etc. supported the focus on application and web design. Many of the security and process flow features are already implemented.
author: Reiner R.
pros: The product can be downloaded, installed, configured and is is ready to log in in under 1h. The steps to do this are clearly described in the manual. All you need is an operating system (Windows server in our case) and a DB plus development environment.The functionality saves the tremendous effort for developing an empty framework of DB access, user management, browser page design etc. Many features are available and there are regular update with more.The integration of the client design system is always soon after the latest releases which allows to update the own application with latest features.The stability / robustness is outstanding. Yet to experience the first failure after 5 years of the application running an an environment with several hundreds of users creating millions of DB records.Extending the developed app or writing plugins is straight-forward. This is due to the well-organized architecture of the overall system.
cons: ASP.NET is not for beginners. What is a beginner and what a pro? This is individual judgement. I found it difficult to take advantage of all the features and sometimes to find out where to implement or change what.This is partly due to my own level (mid experienced but not high), partly due to the sheer complexity and many aspects but also due to the fact that the code has hardly any comments. Sometimes it might make sense to have find some more key features documented in the code.

review_title: Love this software
description: I really enjoy working with ASP.NET Zero as a base for all our webapplications.
author: René V.
pros: What i needed was al well -structured head-start for my applications, so i could focus on functionality
cons: Could use some more starters-kits like CMS, Blog

review_title: Excellent ASP.NET Core / Angular starting point for web applications
description: I am very pleased with ASP.NET Zero. Of course, there are things I wish It either did differently or had for functionality. But it is the best starting point for web applications I have ever found. I fully recommend.
author: Joe L.
pros: I really enjoy developing with ASP.NET Zero. It allowed me to get up and started with some of the more basic features I would put into a web application, such as security, authentication, role management, and a starting point for the front-end using Angular. I fully recommend ASP.NET Zero as an excellent starting point for web applications.
cons: The upgrade process is a bit of a pain point for me. Sometimes they go okay and other times things are broken and it takes me days or even a week or so to get upgraded. There are some features I just don't care for such as Org Units I just have no use for. But I wish It would be easier to get rid of functionality. I wish they were in a plugin or something I could just turn off and remove.

review_title: Great for Enterprise Heavy applications
description: Our team has built 15+ large scale projects using Asp.net zero in the last 6 years. The good points are higher than the bad points and satisfied in terms of large scale app development due to ready framework.
author: shalin J.
pros: I liked the User impersonation, multitenancy settings, rapid development, authentication features the most.
cons: Takes lot of time to load. Even Loading 20 projects in Visual Studio is a pain. Wont suggest it for a small project. Deployment & Coding can be time taking due to so many references.

review_title: An intelligent and mature framework for SaaS and Enterprise App using Asp.Net Core
author: Omkar C.
pros: ASP.NET ZERO is an intelligent and matured framework. it is thoughtfully crafted to provide a perfect foundation for Enterprise & SaaS applications. Customers love this open source framework as it enables faster time to market. With hiretechteam.com, we could deliver key applications based on ASP.NET ZERO in a variety of domains to 25+ global customers successfully
cons: AspNet Zero keeps introducing new version of their framework. each version introduces several new features. it is quite tempting for application developers to incorporate these features into their live app. since there is no auto upgrade, this requires manula upgrade involving experienced developers to undertake this task.

review_title: Simply the best starting point for your next ASP.NET Core web application
description: We create fast to market web applications for our partners and clients using ASP.NET Zero. It's a perfect solution for this. Even first non-functional prototypes looks great from the start.
author: Jeroen G.
pros: The standard features are a welcome starting point. You can start adding your own functionality right away without worrying about authentication, user/role management, logging etc. etc.
cons: Upgrading to a next (major) version can be a pain. You get new functions, that's great. But when there are more fundamental changes you have to be carefully.

review_title: Always with the latest versions
author: Teofilo C.
pros: ASP.NET ZERO has solid documentation that helps you understand how it was built. You stop worrying about what an application should have traditionally and focus only on the business.
cons: Customize the name of the master tables, many institutions have standards that must be met, how the tables should be named, the attributes, the sequences, etc.

review_title: AspNet Zero is the base template to building web applications
description: It a good framework. We use it for our in house application and it saved us many time of development if we had to build many of our must have required features (users management, permission management, adfs integrated for sso ...) from scratch.
author: Tuan T.
pros: ASP.NET Zero provides base architecture with many option for our application (including mobile application), so we just focus on the functionality
cons: - Update to new version is quite difficult and take a lot of time to rework- There some issues about performance if the application have a lot of concurrent users

review_title: Avaliação do produto
description: Em geral a experiência com a ferramenta é muito boa.
author: Paulo B.
pros: Agilidade no desenvolvimento, o multi-tenant
cons: Não permitir alterar as cores do templante e poucas opções de templante.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Go straight to your business needs
author: Salvatore T.
pros: Ready to use and easy to extend. With very little effort we manage multiple contexts for entities from different databases. The strength of this project is the active community it relies on, the clarity and cleanliness of the code, and the online documentation
cons: It would be nice to have compatibility with no-sql databases like mongodb as well

review_title: Helped in Quick starting the project
description: Glad that we chose AspNetZero. It is like hiring a dedicated team of 10 developers working for us for almost free!
author: Ajay K.
pros: Saved at least 6 months of initial effort Repository pattern is handy
cons: No priority support Some suggestions are added in backlog and never got added in milestone

review_title: A clean solution which saves time
author: Lotfi B.
pros: ASP.NET Zero contains the common features we need to create a SaaS solution. You don't need to develop common SaaS features
cons: It will be good if ASP.NET Zero supports Angular material

review_title: Easy to customise with great support
description: Web admin platform, SAAS.
author: Jian Y.
pros: It has quite good documentation, and easy to customise. It provide an excellent base to add functions to fulfil our need.
cons: It has quite frequent updates. So new features are added every some weeks. But it does take some time to upgrade to the newest version.

review_title: System Architect
description: Great product for the money! Even if you don't use the RAD tools, and just use it as a boilerplate on steroids it shines. When building an application, it includes a lot of things people leave out as they are nice to haves, but this product includes a majority of these things out of the box!
author: Tyson G.
pros: When working with the code you soon notice the code is verry verbose and may seem like over kill at times. But the more you look into it you realize it is done just for best practice. This is where ASPNETZERO shines! It is basically a combination of a lot of open-source software that has been curated and assembled to work together properly. This is something most software shops can do and do well. That being said it still takes time & research to do it right, and all resources could be spent for a single app. I'm not sure how many customers ASPNETZERO has, but from the scope of features in the product, the quality of the code, and the amount of documentation available; they have been around the block. I have been using it for three years now and have used it for almost all my new application since.
cons: The generator is nice and easy to use, but to get the most out of it you need to be willing to put in the effort to learn how to modify the generation templates. This is the one place that I feel is more like a black box, there is not a lot of documentation on this, and it takes a little skill, but the payoff is huge that being said.

review_title: The solution
author: Fabrizio D.
pros: I appreciate the continuous update of the framework
cons: I think that it could be faster to retrieve data from the database in a query

review_title: ASP.NET Zero allowed our start-up to quickly achieve production readiness
description: Our experience with the ASP.NET Zero product, Volosoft organisation, and the community, to be overwhelmingly positive.I have, and would continue to, recommend this product to anyway with a need to quickly build a massively functional enterprise application, without sacrificing any of the best practices expected to be found in production-grade software.
author: Adam L.
pros: Most of the infrastructural plumbing has been considered by the architects of ASP.NET Zero, and arranged within the application code-base in a sensible, clean and intuitive fashion. This removes one of the largest hurdles to application development - upskilling new team members to follow a homogenous set of patterns and practices.
cons: Our greatest complaint - which I do see shared from others, is the difficulty in upgrading ASP.NET Zero. We estimate for our product, a 100-150 man hour cost to performing this upgrade - and parallel branches would be best avoided during this time, due to the scale of churn.Given it's nature - source-code rather than just a binary dependency - it's very easy to "mix" our product-specific changes into the ASP.NET Zero authored code.While ASP.NET Zero does support modularisation, which we expect would minimise this - there are some things that just are not possible - or are orders of magnitude more difficult to do in a modular manner.I would love to see a greater adoption of the Open-closed Principle and training documentation/best practices to better mitigate this weakness.

review_title: Great solution to develop quality software easily
description: We're pleased to have chosen ASP.NET Zero to implement or Event Management SaaS, all the features that come out of the box makes a high quality product very easily
author: Emiliano S.
pros: How many features come out of the box to quickly develop a reliable, scalable and easy to maintain solution.
cons: It might have a hard learning curve for beginners to understand all the features that come out of the box and how to customize them. But once you master it, it becomes extremely powerful.

review_title: Super flexible and easy to use .Net platform to build highly advanced products
description: Excellent. We use it for our core SaaS platform and it saved us well over 12 months of development if we had to build many of our must have required features from scratch.
author: Rick F.
pros: It is feature rich and covers all the must haves for a SaaS product, including authentication, authorisation, multi tenancy, auditing, logging and mobile solutions. Uses the latest versions of all technologies and keeps themselves up to date. Best practice templates for the front end as well and easy to extend and expand on where you want to alter or enhance the base platform. Full source code provided and responsive support team if you notice something that doesn't look quite right.
cons: The Metronic UI (in the Angular based solution) takes some getting used to and some of the components aren't the best. We enhanced it however by using Syncfusion components and have a great front end solution.

review_title: ASP.Net in a Transport Solution
description: Secure. No hassles. The learning curve was a bit steep, but it paid off.
author: Theunis V.
pros: Very quick setup. Loads of off the shelf features. Data and row level security. Password reset, expiry and MFA. Developer Tools. SECURITY!
cons: The Metronic Themes are not great, and there is not a lot of research / guidance available. Default themes waste a lot of real estate on wide headers and margins.

review_title: The Perfect Foundation for a SaaS platform
description: I cannot stress enough how much I like this product and would absolutely use this platform in the future. I have even recommended to our development partner that they should look to implement ASP.NET Zero for more of their clients because of how good a platform it is.
author: Sanjay A.
pros: We estimated that the existing out of the box features saved us well over 6 months of development time because they were already well implemented. Multi-Tenancy, the concept of Features, and role-based security were all key to our success.
cons: We found working with the Metronic themes the most problematic, but that is relative since nothing was really terribly difficult. Upgrading to new versions were just more time consuming because of the Metronic themes.

review_title: ASP.NET Zero InsureTech
description: Support is pretty good - usually get a response with 3 working days We have a fairly common architecture (AWS / ELB / ECS / Docker) so most issues we come across are not with ASP.NET Zero as such and have been experienced and resolved already
author: Matt K.
pros: Modular design Separation of functions / services Tech stack integrates easily into our AWS infrastructure environment
cons: Client use of Metronic theme - would love to see alternatives such as Material
